name: Caramel-Pecan Marshmallows
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a cookie sheet with waxed paper.
2. Place the caramels and hot water in the top of a double boiler. Heat over simmering water, stirring often, until the caramels are melted.
3. Using a fork or thick skewer, hold the marshmallow. Dip it into the melted caramel and turn to coat the entire surface. Roll the marshmallow in the finely chopped pecans.
4. Place each marshmallow on the waxed paper. Work quickly to coat each one.
5. Stir the caramel mixture occasionally to keep it smooth. When all are finished, place in a cool place until the caramel is firm.
6. Store in a covered container in a cool place.
Yield: 32 covered marshmallows.
Double this recipe if desired, remembering to work quickly so that the caramel does not cool too soon.
SUBSTITUTE: Toasted coconut can be used for part or all of the chopped nuts if preferred.
